The Create design API now supports email in the name field for preset types.
A new type request parameter has been added to the Create design API. Although this type is shown as required, for backward compatibility, if it isn't
summary: A new type request parameter has been added to the Create design API. Although this type is shown as required, for backward compatibility, if it isn't provided in a request, the request type will be assumed to be type_and_asset.

title: The Create design autofill job API now supports column_configs for chart data fields, allowing you to define column names and data types separately f
summary: The Create design autofill job API now supports column_configs for chart data fields, allowing you to define column names and data types separately from your data.

title: The Create design export job API now supports two additional email export formats: html_bundle and html_standalone. Use these formats when creating e
summary: The Create design export job API now supports two additional email export formats: html_bundle and html_standalone. Use these formats when creating export jobs for email designs, depending on whether you need bundled assets (html_bundle) or a standalone HTML output (html_standalone).

PKCE is now generally available!
summary: We are excited to announce support for Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE). PKCE is an OAuth security extension that enables developers to build more secure Slack applications on public clients, such as desktop apps and mobile apps, without the need to embed a vulnerable client_secret.

title: Release: Slack CLI v3.15.0
summary: Version 3.15.0 of the developer tools for the Slack platform has arrived!

title: Optional scopes have landed
summary: You can now mark individual OAuth scopes as optional when configuring your Slack app! Optional scopes give users more control over what data your app can access during installation, without blocking them from installing your app entirely.

title: Set status method scope update
summary: The assistant.threads.setStatus API method now accepts either the assistantwrite scope. This allows channel-based apps to use AI loading states in channels, without having to request assistantwrite scope in favor of the chat:write scope exclusively, so update your app soon!
